title: SmokeTestExecutorSpec
description:
Input spec for the ``smoke-test-executor`` composite primitive (WS5 #2).

Collapses ``hpc-build-executor``'s inline ``python -c "import argparse, importlib.util, sys; ... m.compute(...)"`` smoke test — the one the skill's own execution-style header forbids (the permission classifier hard-blocks arbitrary ``python -c`` patterns) — into one deterministic CLI verb.

The probe imports the scaffolded executor module from a file path and calls its ``compute(args)`` entry point with a minimal ``argparse.Namespace(output_file=...)``, exactly as the new-contract dispatcher would at runtime, inside a child process so a crashing / exiting module can't take down the CLI.

module_path.title: Module Path
module_path.description: Absolute path to the executor .py file to import and probe. Typically the ``data.path`` returned by the ``build-executor`` primitive.

output_file.title: Output File
output_file.description: Path passed to compute() as Namespace.output_file. Defaults to a throwaway under /tmp; the smoke test only checks the module imports and compute() runs clean, not the artifact it writes.

timeout_sec.title: Timeout Sec
timeout_sec.description: Subprocess timeout for the probe. A module that spins (or blocks on input) is killed at this bound; the result then carries ``timed_out: true`` and ``exit_code: null`` rather than hanging the CLI.
